title: submit-and-verify output data
description:
Shape of the ``data`` field on a successful envelope.

Always carries the submit half; the verify half is None when the
canary was skipped (``submit.canary=False``) or when the submit
was a deduped replay (no fresh canary to wait on).

job_ids.description: Main array job ids. EMPTY when the canary failed verification — the main array never launched (the #160 two-phase gate).
job_ids.title: Job Ids

deduped.description: True when the submit half was a deduped replay.
deduped.title: Deduped

canary_run_id.description: Run id of the canary sibling sidecar. None when canary was skipped (submit.canary=False) or on a deduped replay.
canary_run_id.title: Canary Run Id

canary_job_ids.description: Scheduler ids for the canary. None when canary skipped.
canary_job_ids.title: Canary Job Ids

verified.description: True iff verify-canary returned ok=True — and ONLY then is the main array launched (#160). False on any canary-side failure (main never launches; job_ids empty) AND when verification was skipped (no canary fired / deduped replay).
verified.title: Verified

failure_kind.description: Pass-through from verify-canary. None on success, None when canary was skipped.
failure_kind.title: Failure Kind

verify_result.description: Full verify-canary envelope when verification ran; None when canary was skipped or submit was deduped.

CanaryFailureFeatures.description:
Structured diagnostic evidence attached to a failed canary envelope.

Today (pre-fix) a canary that died cluster-side returned
``{failure_kind: "dispatcher_failed"}`` with no cluster context — the
orchestrator's prose-level "go fetch the log" step was the only path
to the actual error, and agents kept skipping it. This object moves
that step into framework code: the same ``stderr_tail`` is restated
under ``cluster_log_tail`` for structured consumers, and
``classified_error`` carries the
:func:`hpc_agent.ops.recover.failure_signatures.classify` result so a
decision-maker reads an ``error_class`` + ``suggested_fix`` instead
of paraphrasing the log.

cluster_log_tail.description: Raw last ~50 lines of the canary's cluster log, verbatim. The same content as the top-level ``stderr_tail`` field, restated under a structured key so downstream consumers don't have to know which top-level field carries it.
cluster_log_tail.title: Cluster Log Tail

log_path.description: Remote path of the cluster log file the tail was read from, when known. Lets the operator ssh over and tail more if 50 lines isn't enough. ``None`` when no log was fetched.
log_path.title: Log Path

classified_error.description: The ``{error_class, suggested_fix, matched_pattern}`` triple returned by :func:`ops.recover.failure_signatures.classify` on the cluster log tail. ``None`` when the stderr was empty (nothing to classify).
classified_error.title: Classified Error

CanaryFailureFeatures.title: CanaryFailureFeatures

VerifyCanaryResult.description:
Result of the wait + grep + output-check protocol for a 1-task canary.

Caller branches on ``ok``: True → main array submit; False →
surface stderr_tail to the user verbatim.

details.description: One-line human-readable summary the slash command surfaces above the raw stderr_tail.
details.title: Details

stderr_tail.description: Last ~50 lines of the canary's stderr log (or empty string when not retrievable).
stderr_tail.title: Stderr Tail

metrics_fingerprint.description: Optional sha256 of the canary's expected output file when the caller asked for a fingerprint. None on every failure path and when fingerprinting is skipped or fails.
metrics_fingerprint.title: Metrics Fingerprint

failure_features.description: Structured diagnostic evidence attached to failed-canary envelopes (``ok=False``). Carries the raw cluster log tail under a structured key plus a ``classify()`` result against the same failure-signature CATALOG ``ops/recover`` uses, so the orchestrator gets an actionable remediation instead of a bare ``failure_kind``. ``None`` on success.

VerifyCanaryResult.title: verify-canary output
